ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally. The mediastinum and trachea are midline. Lung fields demonstrate hyperinflation; residual scarring and thickening are present. ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally. The mediastinum and trachea are midline. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. Hila are mildly atelectatic. Both diaphragms and costophrenic angles appear normal. No osseous abnormalities are identified in the imaged areas. ground_truth.impression: Lines and tubes. Low lung volumes with infrahilar atelectasis. Recommend clinical correlation for atelectasis management.

ground_truth.findings: The thorax is symmetric bilaterally. The mediastinum and trachea are midline. Lung fields show hypoexpansion; left basilar airspace opacity is present. Cardiac silhouette is borderline enlarged. Hila are not enlarged. Both diaphragms and costophrenic angles appear normal. No osseous abnormalities are identified in the imaged areas. Endotracheal tube is seen ending above the carina. Enteric tube is noted extending below the diaphragm. Right IJ line is noted ending about the mid SVC. No pneumothorax is seen.
ground_truth.impression: 1. Endotracheal tube seen ending above the carina. 2. Lungs hypoexpanded. Left basilar airspace opacity may reflect atelectasis or pneumonia. 3. Vascular congestion and borderline cardiomegaly. Small left pleural effusion suspected. Recommend clinical correlation and possible follow-up imaging for pleural effusion and left basilar opacity.
